Implements a system for receiving and parsing log messages streamed by firmware.

# Purpose
The code defines a logging system for receiving and parsing log messages streamed by firmware. It consists of two main components: the `LogMessage` class and the `StreamingLogs` class. The `LogMessage` class is a named tuple that represents a log message with attributes such as `log_level`, `task`, `timestamp`, `file_name`, `line_number`, and `message`. It includes a method [`__str__`](<#LogMessage__str__>) to format the log message as a string and a class method [`parse`](<#LogMessageparse>) to unpack and decode a binary packet into a `LogMessage` instance.

The `StreamingLogs` class is responsible for managing the reception of log messages. It initializes a socket connection through an interface to receive log data. The [`receive`](<#StreamingLogsreceive>) method uses the `LogMessage.parse` method to convert received packets into `LogMessage` objects. The [`close`](<#StreamingLogsclose>) method terminates the socket connection. This code is intended to be used as part of a larger system where it can be imported and utilized to handle log streaming from firmware, providing a structured way to interpret and display log data.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `__future__.absolute_import`
- `collections`
- `struct`
- `datetime.datetime`

#### LogMessage\.\_\_str\_\_<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.LogMessage.__str__}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.py#L28>)

Formats the log message attributes into a string representation.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Formats the `timestamp` attribute to a string with milliseconds precision and removes the last three digits to get microseconds.
    - Creates a template string that includes `log_level`, `task`, formatted `timestamp`, `file_name`, `line_number`, and `message`.
    - Uses the `format` method to replace placeholders in the template with actual attribute values of the `LogMessage` instance.
- **Output**: A formatted string that represents the log message with its attributes.
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.LogMessage`](<#LogMessage>)  (Base Class)

#### LogMessage\.parse<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.LogMessage.parse}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.py#L35>)

Parses a binary packet into a `LogMessage` object by unpacking and decoding its components.
- **Decorators**: `@classmethod`
- **Inputs**:
    - `packet`: A binary packet that contains log message data to be parsed.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Unpacks the binary `packet` using the `response_struct` format defined in the `LogMessage` class.
    - Extracts and decodes the `log_level`, `task`, `timestamp`, `file_name`, and `line_number` from the unpacked data.
    - Decodes the remaining part of the `packet` as a UTF-8 string to get the `msg`.
    - Creates and returns a new `LogMessage` object with the extracted and decoded data.
- **Output**: A `LogMessage` object containing the parsed log message data.
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.LogMessage`](<#LogMessage>)  (Base Class)

#### StreamingLogs\.\_\_init\_\_<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.StreamingLogs.__init__}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.py#L55>)

Initializes a `StreamingLogs` instance by opening a socket on a specified port number using the provided interface.
- **Inputs**:
    - `interface`: An object that provides a `simplex_transport` attribute with an `open_socket` method.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Attempts to open a socket using the `simplex_transport` of the provided `interface` with the port number defined by `PORT_NUMBER`.
    - If the `interface` does not have the required `simplex_transport` attribute, raises a `TypeError` indicating that the `LoggingApp` must be bound directly to an `Interface`, not a `Link`.
- **Output**: None
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.StreamingLogs`](<#StreamingLogs>)  (Base Class)

#### StreamingLogs\.receive<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.StreamingLogs.receive}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.py#L63>)

Receives a log message from a socket and parses it into a `LogMessage` object.
- **Inputs**:
    - `block`: A boolean that specifies if the receive operation should block until a message is available.
    - `timeout`: An optional timeout value in seconds for the receive operation.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the `receive` method on the `socket` object with `block` and `timeout` as arguments to get a packet.
    - Passes the received packet to the `LogMessage.parse` method to convert it into a `LogMessage` object.
- **Output**: A `LogMessage` object parsed from the received packet.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.LogMessage.parse`](<#LogMessageparse>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pebble-commander/pebble/commander/apps/streaming_logs.StreamingLogs`](<#StreamingLogs>)  (Base Class)

- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the `close` method on the `socket` attribute of the `StreamingLogs` instance.
- **Output**: No output is returned.
